mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly decline in the unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer spending power, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment remains above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which amplifies earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term marginal predictive power is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and triggers short-term positive equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses to negative returns when inflation expectations exceed 3.0%, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with persistently low policy rates signals impending monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the inflation-rate interaction term shows no significant predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects stable economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations despite positive payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate exceeding 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand, which triggers immediate positive earnings revisions and short-term equity price appreciation over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive 5-day returns in >50% of triggered instances, or predictive accuracy vanishes when GDP revisions exceed 0.5% between vintage and final estimates.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates productivity-driven growth without wage-push inflation, which sustains corporate margins and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns when unemployment declines slower than 0.2pp per month, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.
